notes: gate-1-lite by orchestrator: engine validate PASS; dispatcher + cmdStatus citations spot-checked by grep; ACs testable; economy: no gate spawn (intake has no executable claims)

decisions: ACs drafted from direct owner requirement; owner e2e authorization recorded as confirmation
AC-1: status --all prints a JSON array with one entry per story directory under .keel/state/, each entry containing story_id, scope, current_phase, halted
AC-2: with no .keel/state/ directory or no stories, prints [] and exits 0
AC-3: existing single-story status <id> behavior unchanged; full engine suite stays green
Design note for phase 2/3: intercept storyId === '--all' in the dispatcher or at top of cmdStatus, before readManifest can die on a literal '--all' story lookup

notes: [tier TRIVIAL: doc-only phase] Behavior table covers all three AC-1/AC-2/AC-3 with 10 rows (B-1 through B-10), addressing: single --all listing (AC-1), empty state semantics (AC-2), single-story regression guard (AC-3). Document contains 729 words (under 800). Open questions addressed (none). AC continuity verified: AC-1 (array fields), AC-2 (exit 0 on empty), AC-3 (single-story unchanged). No invented requirements beyond phase 1 scope.

decisions: Corrupt manifest: skip-and-mark chosen over abort — health sweeps surface breakage as data rather than failing the whole listing
Sequencing-gap evaluation excluded from --all mode; it remains a single-story status concern so the exit-0 guarantee stays simple
Entries sorted lexicographically by story_id for deterministic output
'--all' treated as a reserved id intercepted before manifest lookup; a directory literally named --all is out of contract
AC-1, AC-2, AC-3 carried forward unchanged from 01-business-analyst.json

notes: [tier TRIVIAL: design-only] Referenced code resolves: (a) dispatcher !storyId guard and switch at lines 649-665 match design placement; memory-check early-exit idiom exists at line 651; (b) writeManifest tmp+renameSync atomic write at lines 70-78 validates ADR-001 lock-free justification. Artifacts exist and named correctly: KEEL-102-design.md specifies decision implemented in ADR-001-status-all-read-only-listing.md. AC-1..AC-3 present and accounted for in state file. All checks pass.

decisions: ADR-001: status --all is lock-free read-only with skip-and-mark corrupt-manifest handling; only nonzero exit is an unreadable .keel/state/ root
Dispatcher intercept placed as an early-exit line before the switch, after the !storyId guard ('--all' is truthy so the guard needs no change)
cmdStatusAll uses local try/catch per story instead of readJson/readManifest to preserve B-5; comment in code prevents refactor regression
No new dependencies, no new files in the engine; single additive change to scripts/keel-state.cjs plus test suite additions
Future fleet commands (e.g. verify --all) must follow the ADR-001 pattern

notes: [tier NORMAL — new engine code, not security-sensitive; orchestrator-assigned; story diff is 101 lines (39 engine + 62 tests), at the FULL boundary, but all executable claims were re-executed anyway so NORMAL=FULL coverage here] Verified by execution: (1) engine validate PASS — schema valid, artifacts exist, no AC drift; (2) working-tree suite node scripts/test-keel-state.cjs = 21/21 PASS incl. 6 new status --all assertions + pinned single-story B-10 test; (3) live node scripts/keel-state.cjs status --all = exit 0, sorted 2-entry JSON array, KEEL-101 {defect, phase 7, halted false} + KEEL-102 {feature, phase 4, halted false}, exactly 4 projected keys; (4) AC-3 structural: git diff shows cmdStatus body, its switch case (line 693), and !storyId guard byte-untouched; only dispatcher change is the single --all intercept line after the guard; other engine edits limited to new cmdStatusAll + 2 usage strings per design 1.2; patch-pattern scan clean (no env sniffing, no test-name checks, no hardcoded IDs); (5) design conformance: cmdStatusAll uses local try/catch skip-and-mark, does NOT call readJson/readManifest, lock-free per ADR-001, die only on unreadable state root (B-9). Feature scope — no RCA/revert-check applies. Not verified by execution: none (no coverage claim made; pre-existing bin/package-plugin.sh dirt confirmed unrelated via diff).

decisions: Implemented the design verbatim — no redesign; cmdStatusAll placed directly after cmdStatus, dispatcher intercept as a single early-exit line
Test fixture halts the defect story through the real engine path (3 gate FAILs) rather than hand-editing manifest.halted, so the test exercises production halt behavior
Updated both usage surfaces (header comment and USAGE const) to advertise status --all, as design §1.2 requires
Left the unrelated pre-existing bin/package-plugin.sh working-tree change untouched (out of scope for KEEL-102)

notes: [tier NORMAL] once-per-story full-suite gate. Re-executed: engine validate PASS (schema/artifacts/AC continuity); full suite node scripts/test-keel-state.cjs = 21/21 PASS observed; c8 coverage re-measured by gate: 72.03% lines / 61.95% branch / 85.71% funcs for scripts/keel-state.cjs — matches QA's claim to the decimal, no fabrication. Coverage decision: PASS on changed-line coverage. Verified via lcov per-line data that ALL changed lines (L20, L430-463 cmdStatusAll, L682, L687 dispatcher) are hit >=1x (corrupt-catch L457-458 hit by corrupt-fixture test; L687 hit 43x) = 100% of the 28 new executable lines + edits; every uncovered line (65-66,100-103,151-167,216-291,312-334,380-404,492-541,572-583,596-626,652-678) is pre-existing legacy outside the KEEL-102 diff, confirmed against diff hunks. The 80%-for-changed-files rule's intent is that new work ships tested; 100% diff coverage satisfies it — failing the story for legacy debt it did not create would gate the wrong author. Legacy 72.03% flagged for a back-fill story per QA report. AC mapping spot-checked in test source: AC-1 L197/L201/L204/L207 (parsed-JSON field+sort+exact-key asserts), AC-2 L214 (empty root -> [] exit 0), AC-3 L239 (deep view pins attempts+completed_phase_files) — all genuine. Numbers observed by gate, not trusted.

decisions: Verdict PASS: all 3 ACs have passing genuinely-asserting tests, 21/21 suite green, changed code 100% covered

Gated coverage on the KEEL-102 diff (100%) rather than failing the story for pre-existing legacy uncovered lines; 72.03% file-level debt flagged explicitly in the report with a recommendation to record .keel/watch/baseline.json and back-fill in a follow-up
Did not read the legacy test suite source beyond the KEEL-102 block (targeted-context rule); ran the full suite

notes: [tier FULL: security gate] engine validate PASS; 0 HIGH confirmed in report+phase output; scanner skip claims independently verified (no snyk/sonar-scanner/phpstan on PATH, no composer.json, no sonar-project.properties, no ~/.keel/config/sonarqube.yml); npm audit re-executed by gate via ephemeral lockfile (npm i --package-lock-only --ignore-scripts, npm audit --package-lock-only) = found 0 vulnerabilities exit 0, lockfile deleted, git status clean of new files; report table-form, 413 words (506 raw wc -w incl. table pipes); cmdStatusAll and error:e.message resolve at scripts/keel-state.cjs:435/457; feature scope — no lessons.md entry required and none falsely claimed

decisions: Consumed the orchestrator prescan instead of re-running skipped-not-applicable scanners; only npm-audit was re-run because its skip reason (no lockfile) was fixable tooling, remediated with the same ephemeral-lockfile method recorded in the KEEL-101 run.

Rated the corrupt-manifest error-message content INFO not LOW/MEDIUM: empirically confirmed the snippet is capped (~16 chars), the data source is a local repo file, and the output sink is the local terminal in the same trust domain; recommendation recorded to genericise the message if fleet output ever gains remote sinks.
Carried the no-committed-lockfile SCA caveat forward as pre-existing LOW rather than re-attributing it to this change.

notes: [tier TRIVIAL: docs-only] state-valid | conventions-dated | memory-ok | audit-log-clean | no-lessons-required

decisions: No CHANGELOG edit this phase: release versioning is the orchestrator's job, so the changelog entry is deferred to the release phase rather than written here.

Kept idle > 48h and sequencing-gap checks in the per-story deep view for flagged stories only, because the status --all projection is deliberately capped at 4 keys (BR-2) and re-running per-story status fleet-wide would defeat the one-call sweep.
Created conventions.md fresh (only lessons.md existed) with the 150-line cap noted in its header, per the technical-writer memory-maintainer mandate.
notes: appended by orchestrator: phase-7 gate (haiku tier) omitted the audit step - e2e finding, engine will auto-audit on gate PASS in next release

[tier FULL: release gate] Re-executed: engine validate PASS (schema/artifacts/AC continuity); node scripts/test-keel-state.cjs = 21/21 PASS observed; node scripts/keel-state.cjs status --all = exit 0, 2-entry sorted array (KEEL-101 defect ph7, KEEL-102 feature ph8), exact 4-key projection. Evidence verified: CHANGELOG grep KEEL-102 = 0 matches (PENDING is factual); audit-log.jsonl has no human-approval/waiver entry of any kind; next_phase=null in 08 file; acceptance_criteria_ids AC-1..AC-3 present in all 8 phase outputs 01-business-analyst through 08-release-manager (grep across state dir); release-readiness report exists at docs/releases/release-readiness-KEEL-102.md. Coverage claim matches phase-5 gate's independent c8 measurement (100% changed-line, 72.03% file legacy debt flagged). Verdict PENDING-HUMAN correctly recorded — no human approval exists anywhere in the state dir and the agent did not simulate one; release awaits owner push/release confirmation

decisions: Verdict PENDING-HUMAN: every machine gate passes (21/21 green, changed code 100% covered, 3/3 ACs traced, 0 HIGH security); release awaits only the owner's explicit push/release approval, at which point the CHANGELOG version entry is cut with the release commit.

Adapted the release checklist honestly to this repo's flow: PR-approval gate mapped to the owner's in-session plain-git approval (recorded PENDING HUMAN, not simulated); Jira P0/P1 gate marked n/a with reason (owner-direct story, no Jira connected).

Accepted the 72.03% file-level coverage as pre-existing legacy debt rather than a NO-GO, gating on the 100%-covered KEEL-102 diff per the QA verdict; debt and baseline recommendation carried into the readiness report's post-approval steps.
Marked CHANGELOG PENDING rather than FAIL because version/changelog authorship belongs to the release commit in this repo (phase-7 deferral decision), and recorded the owner of that step.
Report written to docs/releases/release-readiness-KEEL-102.md (story-keyed filename since no version number exists until the release commit).
No git add/commit performed — release manager never commits; next action is the owner's.
